calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
Off Calendar Oct-05-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Brad Gould, via ZOOM, for Calvin Chang, Personal Representative.

The court begins by asking Mr. Gould regarding information regarding the Franchise Tax Board's position. Mr. Gould confirms that attempts have been made to contact the Franchise Tax Board.

The Petition for Final Distribution is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
Off Calendar Sep-15-2021 Continued To Oct-04-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of An Amended Petition Under Probate Code 12251
Off Calendar Sep-15-2021 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jul-26-2021 Continued To Sep-15-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Brad Gould, via ZOOM, for Calvin Chang, via ZOOM, Executor.

The court at the outset confirms with Mr. Gould of his intent to file an amended petition to seek relief under probate code 12251.

The Petition for Final Distribution is continued to September 15, 2021, at 9:00 am in Department 204. Status of filing of an Amended Petition under probate code 12251 is also set for the same date.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Loo. Not reported.

An Order to Appear and Order to Show Cause will be issued to the Executor and the Attorney to appear on March 30, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear on January 27, 2020 and February 24, 2020, why a petition for final distribution or Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Administration has not been filed, why $150.00 sanctions should not be imposed on Counsel and Executor separately, and why the Executor should not be suspended or removed. The status hearing and order to appear are also continued to that date.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: Anasse. Matter not reported.

Off Calendar Aug-01-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Yoshida for Calvin Chang.

The Court noted that no one is here for Vincent. Mr. Yoshida stated that Vincent retained new Counsel, that Vincent is only concerned about the Trust in Los Angeles, that Vincent's petition is really an appeal because he does not agree with the Los Angeles Judge's decision and asked the Court to dismiss Vincent's petition. The Court noted that Vincent filed a Petition to Remove Trustee with a hearing date of October 10, 2018 and that the petition is not before him. The Court also noted that no one is here to object and that the objection filed by Vincent does not carry over to this petition. The Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary (subject to review of the order). Bond is waived. Status on close of estate is set for January 27, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Mar-20-2018 Continued To May-08-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Preston Marx for Calvin Chang via Court Call

The Court inquired why Probate was necessary in San Francisco when all of the assets are in Hawaii. Mr. Marx and his senior paralegal stated that they received an email from a probate attorney in Hawaii indicating that the Hawaii Court is requiring Letters to be issued in California in order to start an ancillary probate in Hawaii, that Hawaii's position is that probate should be issued in the home state, and they would be unable to proceed without California Letters. Mr. Marx requested 30 day continuance to file declaration from Hawaii counsel regarding Hawaii court's requirements. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to May 8, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of said declaration.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Shirley Le, Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.
